purpose: Score and remediate event records before publishing or promoting them.

score_bands.action: Promote heavily, pursue sponsors and partners, and capture case-study data.
Publish only after resolving named weaknesses.
Keep internal until concept, logistics, or economics are rebuilt.
Reject, archive, or fully rebuild before publication.

criteria.label: Audience and positioning
checks: Specific attendee persona is named.
One-sentence attendee promise is clear.
Differentiation from similar events is evident.
Demand evidence is realistic.

criteria.label: Program and experience
checks: Run-of-show or schedule is concrete.
Talent, speakers, artists, performers, cast, judges, or hosts are credited by role.
The event has a clear emotional or experiential arc.
The program includes memorable or repeatable signature moments.

criteria.label: Operational readiness
checks: Venue, date, time, timezone, and capacity are present.
Permits, vendors, staffing, load-in, and production needs are accounted for.
Crowd flow, weather, cancellation, and contingency plans are credible.
The event scale matches the venue and staffing plan.

criteria.label: Safety and accessibility
checks: Age policy is explicit.
ADA-aware ticketing, seating, route, restroom, and accommodation details are present.
Security, medical, emergency communication, and incident-response plans are present.
Harassment, alcohol, crowding, and transport risks are addressed where relevant.

criteria.label: Business case
checks: Revenue streams are named.
Major cost categories are named.
Break-even attendance or revenue is calculated.
Sponsor, partner, local economic, or strategic value is defined.
Conservative, expected, and upside scenarios are considered.

criteria.label: Marketing and community
checks: Marketing channels and conversion path are present.
Partnerships, creators, local groups, or community channels are mapped.
Social proof, past results, or comparable demand is included.
Post-event retention path is defined.

criteria.label: Trust and realism
checks: Title, copy, date, venue, capacity, price, and organizer feel specific and believable.
No generic filler or placeholder copy remains.
Claims can be verified or are framed conservatively.
The event record reads as if a real producer owns it.

criteria.label: Measurement plan
checks: KPIs are named before launch.
Attendee satisfaction or NPS collection is planned.
Sales, attendance, engagement, sponsor, and incident data will be captured as relevant.
An after-action review is planned.

criteria.label: Sustainability and ethics
checks: Waste, transport, vendor, and neighborhood impact are considered.
Inclusion and accessibility are not treated as optional.
Labor, vendor, participant, and contestant treatment is fair.
Community disruption has a mitigation plan.

conference.guidance: Conferences need specific sessions, credible speakers, networking design, and sponsor or exhibitor value.
conference.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a professional conference listing with the attendee persona, concrete agenda, speaker/moderator roster, networking format, venue/date/time, and sponsor or exhibitor value.
conference.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around registrations, sponsorship packages, exhibitor/lead value, group sales, venue/production costs, and renewal or pipeline metrics.

concert.guidance: Concerts need a real artist bill, set timing, production plan, and ticket or VIP economics.
concert.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a concert listing with headliner, supporting artists or DJs, set times, genre, venue/date/time, age policy, access details, and production credibility.
concert.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around ticket tiers, VIP/table packages, merch, bar share, sponsorship, artist/promoter settlement, production costs, and sell-through targets.

circuit_party.guidance: Circuit parties need credible DJs/hosts/entertainers, nightlife production, crowd safety, and VIP/bar economics.
circuit_party.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a nightlife/circuit party listing with DJs, hosts, entertainers, door times, venue flow, age policy, production details, dress or door expectations, and safety/access notes.
circuit_party.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around presales, door sales, VIP/tables, bar share, sponsor fit, promoter list size, staffing/security/medical costs, and repeat-party retention.

play.guidance: Plays need cast, director, rights or playwright context, production readiness, occupancy targets, and audience development.
play.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a theater listing with title, playwright or rights context, director, cast, creative team, runtime, run dates, venue, content advisory, and why this production matters now.
play.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around paid occupancy, ticket tiers, group/school sales, rights or royalty costs, venue/production costs, donor conversion, reviews, and repeat attendance.

competition.guidance: Competitions need eligibility, judging, voting or advancement rules, contestant support, prize value, and audience stakes.
competition.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a competition listing with eligibility, audition or application process, judges, hosts, rounds, voting or advancement rules, prize, contestant support, date/time, and finals stakes.
competition.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around applications, ticketed rounds, sponsors, livestream/content value, voting engagement, prize costs, production costs, audience retention, and winner outcomes.

festival.guidance: Festivals need a lineup, site plan, vendor model, sponsor value, safety plan, and local economic rationale.
festival.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a festival listing with lineup, stage or zone schedule, hosts, performers, vendors, site map, venue/date/time, access details, transport, and weather/safety plan.
festival.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around tickets, sponsorship, vendor fees, concessions, merch, parking, grants/tourism partners, site operations, security/medical costs, and local economic impact.

workshop.guidance: Workshops need a concrete learning outcome, instructor credibility, materials plan, and small-capacity economics.
workshop.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a workshop listing with instructor, learner level, concrete takeaway, modules or exercises, materials, capacity, venue/date/time, and accessibility details.
workshop.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around registration price, capacity, instructor fee, materials, venue costs, upsell or membership path, completion, satisfaction, and referral metrics.

market.guidance: Markets need vendor mix, booth economics, foot-traffic strategy, and vendor sales measurement.
market.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a market listing with vendor mix, booth/stall layout, featured makers or food, venue/date/time, shopping experience, access details, and community draw.
market.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around booth or vendor fees, sponsor/concession revenue, expected foot traffic, vendor sales, setup/security/cleanup costs, and repeat vendor retention.

fundraiser.guidance: Fundraisers need a fundraising goal, donor pathway, sponsor/table model, and post-event donor conversion plan.
fundraiser.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a fundraiser listing with cause, goal, host, speakers or performers, honoree if any, donor pathway, table/sponsor options, venue/date/time, and emotional call to action.
fundraiser.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around donation goal, sponsor/table packages, auction or pledge mechanics, donor acquisition, event costs, conversion, and post-event stewardship.

community.guidance: Community events need local relevance, partner ownership, low-risk operations, and community impact measurement.
community.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a community event listing with local audience, host organization, activities, partners, venue/date/time, access details, and neighborhood benefit.
community.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around grants, sponsors, partner contributions, vendor or donation revenue, budget control, volunteer/staffing needs, attendance, and community impact.

other.guidance: Other events still need a clear program, owner, economics, and measurement plan.
other.rewrite_prompt: Rewrite as a concrete event listing with audience, host, format, venue/date/time, credited participants, access details, and measurable attendee value.
other.business_case_prompt: Build the business case around revenue, costs, break-even, partner value, operational risk, and post-event measurement.
